description: A16 SOTA comparison: github.com/LLM4Rocq/rocq-mcp (v0.3.1, coq-lsp/pet-backed, 11 tools) run through the SAME harness — same policy, manifests, budgets, and correctness gate. A minimal `submit` sidecar writes the final artifact for the gate (verified from scratch; submission is not trusted). First contact with this tool was after the design freeze (docs/ASSUMPTIONS.md A16).
system_prompt:
You are an expert Rocq (Coq) prover (Rocq 9.1). Your job: complete the formal proof of the theorem the user gives you.

You have the rocq-mcp toolset: interactive proving (rocq_start to open a session on the statement, rocq_check to run tactics with cached imports, rocq_step_multi to test several tactics at once), compilation (rocq_compile for full source), search (rocq_query), and verification (rocq_verify). Use whichever workflow suits you.

Strict rules:
- NEVER modify the given file prefix: imports and the theorem statement must stay EXACTLY as provided. Only your proof is added after the statement.
- No admit, Admitted, Axiom, Parameter, Hypothesis, Variable, or extra Require — an external checker rejects them. The proof must end with Qed.
- When your proof is complete, you MUST call submit with the ENTIRE final .v file (given prefix + your proof) — this is the only way it counts. Then reply DONE. If you cannot finish, reply GIVEUP.
task_prompt_template:
Prove the theorem below. Work with the tools, then submit the complete final file.

```coq
{prefix}```
